title: US launches additional attacks against Iran as tension escalates in the Middle East
summary: The military escalation includes bombing of railway bridges and threats of retaliation, while Trump insists that the war will not resume.

title: Wall Street falls on fears of military escalation in the Middle East
summary: The Dow Jones loses more than 800 points intraday amid the breakdown of the ceasefire between the US and Iran.

title: Japan's 10-year bond yield hits 30-year high due to inflation and Middle East tensions
summary: The benchmark interest rate exceeds 2.88% amid expectations that the Bank of Japan will adjust its monetary policy.

title: NATO concludes summit urging members to increase defense spending under pressure from Trump
summary: The United States demands greater European contribution, while South Korea strengthens its cooperation with the Alliance.

title: Japanese police and three e-commerce giants to share data to prevent credit card fraud
summary: Mercari, LINE Yahoo, and Rakuten will collaborate with authorities in detecting suspicious transactions.

title: Various 2026 World Cup news: Egypt demands referees be expelled, Argentina advances, Switzerland reaches quarterfinals
summary: Justin Bieber will perform at halftime of the final; Argentina comes back against Egypt with an inspired Messi.

title: Major League November: Okamoto hits his 21st home run, Verlander announces retirement, Dodgers beat Rockies
summary: Pitcher Justin Verlander, three-time Cy Young winner, retires after 266 wins.

title: Weather alert in Japan: Typhoon 9 approaches Okinawa islands, temperatures up to 37°C expected in Kyushu
summary: Authorities issue warnings for torrential rain and heatstroke in wide areas of the country.

title: Shohei Ohtani reaches 300 home runs in MLB, a historic milestone for a Japanese player
summary: The Angels phenomenon achieves the round number combining power hitting and pitching ability.

title: Assembly of floats for Gion Festival in Kyoto begins, with 23 structures planned for July 14
summary: One of the floats, O'funaboko, renews its ornaments with carvings of crows and rabbits.

title: Two teenagers arrested for cyberattack on QuickPal internet cafe chain
summary: Police arrest an 18-year-old and a 12-year-old minor for the leak of member data.

title: Psychiatrists prescribe diabetes drug Mounjaro for weight loss purposes in Japan
summary: The drug, approved for type 2 diabetes, is requested by patients seeking to lose weight, generating medical controversy.

title: Ex-prosecutor from Tokyo special unit investigated for alleged inappropriate relationship with a female suspect
summary: Judicial authorities examine whether the prosecutor had improper contacts with a woman under investigation.

title: Organ trafficking network: an intermediary charged over 45 million yen for four transplants
summary: A suspect received illegal fees in exchange for facilitating organ donation to Japanese patients abroad.

title: Toyama Airport adopts nickname 'Toyama-Takayama Sushi Airport' to attract tourists
summary: The new name aims to boost foreign tourism in the region, although it generates divided reactions among Gifu residents.

title: IOC lifts restrictions on Russia in international competitions, Ukraine condemns decision
summary: The measure allows Russian athletes to participate in the Olympics under a neutral flag, generating controversy.

title: Japan expands drone ban to facilities where the Emperor and Prime Minister are staying
summary: The new regulation includes outdoor areas where senior state officials are present to prevent security incidents.

title: Debate in Japan: Prime Minister suggests eliminating temporary consumption tax reduction in autumn 2027
summary: The government considers the possibility of not extending the VAT cut, while the opposition criticizes the lack of alternatives.

title: Oil rises due to Middle East tensions and Russian diesel export ban
summary: Brent crude exceeds $76 on fears of supply disruption from the Strait of Hormuz and shortage in Russia.

title: South Korea strengthens cooperation with NATO and calls for solutions to the problem of Korean war criminals
summary: South Korean president meets with NATO Secretary General, while families of B and C class convicts request support from Japan.
